template_description: Social-ecological system template for offshore wind energy development, covering construction and operation pressures on marine ecosystems, energy production benefits, and spatial planning responses.

connections.description: Government targets directly drive massive investment in offshore wind construction
Monopile installation generates high-intensity impulsive noise harmful to cetaceans

Industrial sandeel fishing reduces prey availability for surface-feeding seabirds. Fishing mortality above sustainable levels reduces spawning stock biomass, lowering reproductive output and population resilience
Sandeel shortage causes chick starvation and breeding failure in kittiwakes and puffins
Overfishing directly reduces sandeel biomass in the North Sea. Fishing mortality above sustainable levels reduces spawning stock biomass, lowering reproductive output and population resilience
Pile-driving noise causes harbour porpoise displacement and potential hearing damage
Beam trawls physically scrape and flatten seabed habitats. Bottom trawling physically disrupts benthic habitat structure, removing biogenic features.

Sandeel abundance supports both direct fishery and prey base for commercial fish. Fishing mortality above sustainable levels reduces spawning stock biomass, lowering reproductive output and population resilience

Closure zones protect sandeel spawning and feeding areas from fishing mortality. Fishing mortality above sustainable levels reduces spawning stock biomass, lowering reproductive output and population resilience

Bubble curtains reduce noise propagation during pile-driving by 10-20 dB. The negative feedback reflects a trade-off or antagonistic interaction where increases in the source variable suppress or reduce the target through competitive exclusion, resource limitation, or regulatory mechanisms
Industrial demand for fishmeal and fish oil drives sandeel fishery targeting and catch volumes
Wind farm exclusion zones preventing beam trawling is well-documented in North Sea. Multiple studies confirm fisheries displacement from wind farm areas. Confidence raised from 3 to 4.
Sandeel availability is the critical prey resource determining breeding success for most North Sea seabird colonies

Fish stock productivity directly determines fishing fleet revenue and coastal community employment. Fishing mortality above sustainable levels reduces spawning stock biomass, lowering reproductive output and population resilience
Spatial closure zones directly exclude sandeel fishing from key areas, reducing total industrial catch
Maritime spatial planning designates areas for wind energy and nature protection that exclude beam trawling

Chronic hydrocarbon contamination from oil/gas platforms harms sandeel through toxic effects on eggs, larvae, and prey organisms. Polarity corrected from + to - (contamination reduces, not increases, sandeel abundance).
Bubble curtains are the standard technical measure for pile-driving noise mitigation requirements
Habitat compensation schemes are part of maritime spatial planning EIA requirements
Fishing industry employment and revenue reinforces industrial-scale fisheries demand
Energy security and carbon reduction benefits reinforce offshore wind energy policy targets
Decommissioning cost burden accelerates transition away from oil and gas production
Renewable energy generation capacity reinforces offshore wind energy policy targets
